text: A jug contains 1 litre of orange juice. Sam pours out half of the juice. How many millilitres of orange juice are left in the jug?
generatorSolution:
Step 1: Convert 1 litre to millilitres. 1 litre = 1000 ml.
Step 2: Sam pours out half of the juice. Half of 1000 ml = 1000 ÷ 2 = 500 ml poured out.
Step 3: Amount left = 1000 ml − 500 ml = 500 ml.
The correct answer is 500 ml.
